metadata.name: ai.quickdeploy/youtube
metadata.title: YouTube
metadata.description: Generated YouTube channel video Atom feed MCP catalog manifest.

refreshMinutes.description: Background polling interval in minutes for refreshing the feed.

maxItems.description: Maximum number of feed items retained per channel.

includeContent.description: Whether to include full item description/content text in query responses.

notes: YouTube publishes a public per-channel Atom feed at https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id={channelId} (playlist_id and legacy user query params are also supported); this is a long-standing, stable YouTube platform feature rather than a page in Google's formal API developer docs, so it is cited here as a stable, widely-used public endpoint rather than a versioned developer-portal document.

Confirmed live via a direct fetch of the reference channel feed (channel_id=UC_x5XG1OV2P6uZZ5FSM9Ttw, "Google for Developers"): root <feed> element declares the yt (http://www.youtube.com/xml/schemas/2015) and media (http://search.yahoo.com/mrss/) namespace extensions over the base Atom namespace; each <entry> carries yt:videoId, yt:channelId, title, link, published, updated, author, and a media:group block (media:title, media:content, media:thumbnail, media:description, media:community/statistics).
The feed is public and read-only; no API key or authentication is required.

The importer's config schema only supports refreshMinutes/maxItems/includeContent (no per-channel override field), so the channel is pinned via the committed source.uri (channel_id=UC_x5XG1OV2P6uZZ5FSM9Ttw, the reference "Google for Developers" channel used to validate the feed shape above); pointing this manifest at a different channel means editing source.uri.

network: GET https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=UC_x5XG1OV2P6uZZ5FSM9Ttw for the pinned channel's public video feed (read-only, no auth)
process: Run pnpm build/test scripts only inside OpenShell-backed MXC isolation
Run node only as invoked by generated project package scripts inside OpenShell-backed MXC isolation
